$description: Lolly starter brand tokens — the neutral, OKLCH-native DTCG document shipped with the blank lolly-start profile. It doubles as the reference example of the brand token contract (plans/brand-token-contract.md): three ramps + a spectrum in a shared 'base' set, and per-theme semantic slots that alias into them. Every $value is a CSS oklch() string (L%, chroma, hue) so lightness steps are perceptually even; anything needing hex goes through the engine's colorToHex. Anchor: primary = oklch(60% 0.10 250), scheme mono. Replace wholesale via the #/start wizard or 'npm run ingest:brand'.

ramp.$description: Nine-step ramps, 1 = darkest to 9 = lightest, on the shared L scale [18, 28, 38, 50, 62, 74, 84, 92, 97]% with chroma following a bell over lightness (fullest mid-ramp, tapering at both ends). Step 5 of the primary ramp is pulled to the anchor's own L (60%) so the exact brand color sits in its ramp.

primary.$description: The brand ramp — hue 250 (a calm slate blue) at up to 0.10 chroma. Step 5 is the anchor color itself.

1.$description: Deep brand ink — dark-theme on-primary, near-black fills

2.$description: Dark emphasis fills and hover states on light surfaces

3.$description: Strong accent for small text and icons on light surfaces

4.$description: Mid-dark accent — buttons that need light text

5.$description: THE brand color (the anchor) — light-theme primary

6.$description: Lifted accent — dark-theme primary (8.2:1 on the dark surface)

8.$description: Pale tint — washes and large decorative shapes

neutral.$description: Quiet grays that carry the primary hue (250) at ≤ 0.02 chroma, so 'gray' UI still feels like the brand. Surfaces, text, hairlines all come from here.

2.$description: Raised dark surface, strong secondary text on light

3.$description: Dark-theme hairlines and edges (1.9:1 on the dark surface)

4.$description: Light-theme muted text (5.5:1 on the light surface)

5.$description: True mid-gray — disabled states, placeholder text

6.$description: Dark-theme muted text (8.2:1 on the dark surface)

7.$description: Light-theme hairlines and edges (1.5:1 on the light surface)

8.$description: Subtle fills — cards, wells, input backgrounds

secondary.$description: The supporting accent. Scheme is mono, so this is a low-chroma sibling of the primary (same hue 250, chroma × 0.35) — a distinct ramp, not a re-alias, so tools that reach for 'secondary' get a genuinely quieter color.

3.$description: Strong supporting accent on light surfaces

spectrum.$description: Six fixed chart/illustration hues at matched lightness (65%) and moderate chroma (0.12), each nudged 8° toward the primary hue (250) so the set reads as tinted by the brand. Blue sits on the primary hue itself.

semantic.$description: The seven semantic slots for the light look (the default theme — light is first in $themes). Every slot aliases a ramp step; WCAG 2.1 ratios were computed against the resolved sRGB values and honor the contract floors: text ≥ 7.0, muted ≥ 3.0, on-primary ≥ 4.5, edge ≥ 1.3.

primary.$description: The brand color — the anchor itself

on-primary.$description: Text/icons on primary — deep brand ink, 4.8:1 on primary (white only reaches 3.9:1 here, so the ramp end wins)

secondary.$description: Supporting accent for fills and badges

semantic.$description: The seven semantic slots for the dark look — the light theme reversed across the neutral ramp, with primary lifted one step so it keeps reading on the dark surface. Same contract floors, same computed-ratio discipline.

primary.$description: The brand color lifted for dark surfaces — 8.2:1 on surface

on-primary.$description: Text/icons on primary — deep brand ink, 8.2:1 on the lifted primary
